title: crotalaria fascicularis
intro: Crotalaria fascicularis, also known as Stickweed or Bottlebrush, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its health benefits include reducing swelling and promoting wound healing, making it a popular herbal remedy in some cultures. Herbal preparations of Crotalaria fascicularis can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used topically or internally to address various health issues.
